senses.gloss: to be useful, to be profitable
to make the circumstances fit, to reconcile
to be favorable to, to assist, to grant success to [with accusative ‘someone’, along with لِ (li) or إِلَى (ʔilā) ‘in something’] (said of God)
to bring about an agreement between, to reconcile

examples.text: إِن يُرِيدَا إِصْلَاحًا يُوَفِّقِ اللّٰهُ بَيْنَهُمَا

senses.gloss: verbal noun of وَفِقَ (wafiqa, “to fit”) (form I)

examples.text: وجاء إطلاق سراحهم وفق البيان، نتيجة «عملية مشتركة بين شعبة المعلومات في قوى الأمن الداخلي والأمن العام (اللبناني) والمخابرات العراقية». وأسفرت العملية التي نفذتها المخابرات العراقية وفق البيان عن «اعتقال بعض الخاطفين وقتل أحدهم خلالها، وتتم ملاحقة بقية أفراد العصابة».
examples.english: And their release came, according to the statement, as a result of “joint operation between the Information Division of the Internal Security Forces and the General Security of Lebanon and the Iraqi intelligence.” And the operation which the Iraqi intelligence carried out, led, according to the statement, to “the arrest of a deal of the kidnappers and to the killing of one of them during the course of it, and the pursuit of the rest of the individuals of the gang is proceeding.”

etymology.text:
Causative of وَفِقَ (wafiqa, “to fit”).

Adverbial accusative of وَفْق (wafq).
